_comment: Polygon: list of (x, y) corners in meters, going around the room. For non-convex rooms (L-shapes etc.), the order matters — list corners as you'd walk along the wall. The legacy {width_m, height_m} form is still accepted as a rectangle. `txs` is a list; for now you have one transmitter, but the schema supports more without changes.
rxs._comment: Edit MACs to match your boards (run.py heatmap prints unknown ones on first packet) and (x_m, y_m) to match your room. Coordinates in meters; origin doesn't matter — only relative positions. 'txs' is a list; for a single transmitter use a one-element list. 'room' may be either width_m/height_m or a 'polygon' of [x,y] pairs.
